skill.name: Speech-to-Text — verging.ai
skill.description: Transcribe audio to text using OpenAI Whisper through the verging.ai proxy API.
skill.summary: Transcribe audio files to text using OpenAI Whisper via the verging.ai API.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: The skill contains only documentation files (README.md, SKILL.md) with no executable code. All 12 static findings are false positives triggered by markdown-formatted bash examples, documentation URLs, and API key environment variable references. The 'dangerous combination' heuristic fired because the documentation describes a legitimate API workflow involving shell commands, network calls, and credentials. This is expected and appropriate for any API documentation skill.

medium_findings.title: Documentation references API key setup via environment variable
medium_findings.description: The README documents setting VERGING_API_KEY via 'export' command. While this is standard documentation practice, users should be aware that hardcoding API keys in shell history or scripts is a risk. The documentation itself follows best practice by recommending environment variables rather than hardcoding.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks contain shell command examples
low_findings.description: The README contains bash code blocks showing npx, export, and curl commands. These are documentation examples, not executable code. No actual code execution risk exists since this is a pure documentation skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API endpoint URL in documentation
low_findings.description: The verging.ai API endpoint (https://verging.ai/api/v1/ai/stt) is shown in a curl example. This is expected and necessary for API documentation. No security concern as the URL points to a legitimate service.

content.user_title: Transcribe Audio to Text with AI
content.value_statement: Converting audio recordings to text manually is slow and error-prone. This skill provides a simple API to transcribe audio files using OpenAI Whisper, returning accurate text in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Transcribe audio files (MP3, WAV, M4A, WebM) to text using OpenAI Whisper
Support automatic language detection or specify a language code
Handle files up to 25 MB in size
Return transcription with metadata (language, duration, credits consumed)
Authenticate requests via API key in Authorization header
Provide JSON response format with transcribed text
limitations: Maximum audio file size is 25 MB
Requires a verging.ai API key with sufficient credits
Only supports audio formats listed in the API documentation
Transcription quality depends on audio clarity and background noise

use_cases.title: Meeting and interview transcription
use_cases.description: Convert recorded meetings or interviews into searchable text documents for note-taking and archiving.

use_cases.title: Voice memo conversion
use_cases.description: Transform voice memos and audio notes into written text for easy reference and sharing.

use_cases.title: Podcast and video captioning
use_cases.description: Generate transcripts and captions for podcasts or video content to improve accessibility and SEO.

prompt_templates.title: Basic audio transcription
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe the audio file at /path/to/recording.mp3 to text.

prompt_templates.title: Transcribe with specific language
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe the audio file at /path/to/audio.wav. The language is Spanish (es).
prompt_templates.scenario: When you know the audio language

prompt_templates.title: Batch transcription
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe all MP3 files in the /recordings/ directory and save the text output to transcriptions.json.

prompt_templates.title: Transcribe with custom parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe /path/to/podcast.mp3 with temperature 0.2 and response format as plain text.

output_examples.output: Transcribed text of the meeting content, language: en, duration: 300 seconds, credits consumed: 5
Full transcript in Spanish text, language: es, duration: 900 seconds, credits consumed: 15

best_practices: Store the API key in an environment variable, never hardcode it in scripts
Verify audio file format and size before uploading to avoid errors
Specify the language parameter when known to improve accuracy
Monitor credit usage and set up alerts for unexpected consumption
anti_patterns: Do not commit API keys to version control or share them publicly
Do not attempt to upload files larger than 25 MB without compressing them first
Do not rely solely on automatic language detection for critical transcriptions

faq.question: What audio formats are supported?
faq.answer: The API supports MP3, MP4, MPEG, MPGA, M4A, WAV, and WebM formats up to 25 MB.

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Visit verging.ai, log in, click your avatar in the top right, and create an API key from the API Keys section.

faq.question: How much does transcription cost?
faq.answer: Transcription costs 1 credit per minute of audio, with a minimum charge of 1 credit per request.

faq.question: Can I specify the language?
faq.answer: Yes, use the language parameter with a language code such as en, es, zh, or ja for better accuracy.

faq.question: What is the maximum file size?
faq.answer: The maximum audio file size is 25 MB. Files exceeding this limit will be rejected with a 400 error.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
